primary_variables.matched_definition: Comparison between disease and matched control cohorts.
evidence: analyzed the protein turnover of differential AS groups across multiple HeLa cell lines collected from different laboratories

The remaining twelve cell lines represented six HeLa cells variants subtype CCL2 (2, 6, 7, 12, 13, and 14) and six HeLa cells variants subtype Kyoto (1, 3, 4, 8, 9, and 10), presenting a balanced comparison between HeLa Kyoto and CCL2 strains.

matched_class_path: gene modification or gain of function
primary_variables.matched_definition: Base or amino-acid substitution (for example SNV, point mutation, engineered missense).
evidence: These HeLa cell lines were shown to harbor a considerable heterogeneity on mRNA, protein, and protein degradation stemming from copy number variations (CNV) accumulated due to genomic instability and clonal effects
Cells were harvested and counted in two biological replicates at four different time points (0, 1, 4.5, and 11 h).

replicate_hints: two biological replicates at four different time points
Two dishes of whole-process replicate were prepared at each time
multiplex_channel_hints: pSILAC (pulse stable isotope-labeled amino acids in cells)
SILAC labels (“Arg10” and “Lys8”) were specified in the second channel

evidence: relative correlation of the fold changes per gene may directly reflect the significance of the post-transcriptional regulation between different conditions
relative difference of k loss between HeLa CCL2 and Kyoto enriched distinctive GOBPs and GOCCs
The RNA-Seq data for HeLa 12 (variant CCL2, passage 50; P50) were compared to the data from HeLa 14 (variant CCL2, passage 7; P7).

base_candidates_ranked.reason: The study explicitly uses data-independent acquisition mass spectrometry (DIA-MS) for both total protein abundance and pSILAC measurements.
DDA was used for library generation and the experiment utilizes pSILAC labeling.

modifier_candidates_ranked.reason: The core of the study is measuring protein degradation rates (k loss) using pulse stable isotope-labeled amino acids in cells (pSILAC).
The study focuses on isoform-resolved analysis using a sample-specific RNA-Seq-derived protein sequence database to identify alternative splicing (AS) proteoforms.
